title: DORIS — Shared Definitions
description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions shared across NYC Department of Records & Information Services (DORIS) object schemas. Reconciles fields common to the DORIS Open Data datasets (data.cityofnewyork.us), nyc.gov/site/records, the Municipal Archives collections (archives.nyc / Preservica DAMS at nycrecords.access.preservica.com, formerly LUNA Imaging), the Historical Vital Records portal (a860-historicalvitalrecords.nyc.gov), and the OpenRecords FOIL system. Includes the NYC geography spine, the borough↔county mapping used by historical vital records, and the shared coordinate shape.

x-nyc-commons.note: Geography/identifier/address/money definitions are $ref'd from the canonical nyc-commons set; agency-specific definitions remain local.

Borough.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/Borough); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Borough still resolve.

County.description: County of record used on historical vital records (the pre-consolidation civil registers) — the legal county name for each borough.

Coordinates.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/Coordinates); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Coordinates still resolve.

Geography.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/GeographySpine); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Geography still resolve.

Provenance.description: Where the digitized object or record actually lives — the vendor DAMS and portal surfaces DORIS points to (the object itself is not in Open Data).

system.description: Which access system holds or serves the object.

url.description: Public access URL for the object, if any.
